summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The Cluster class provides a way to access information about the map/reduce cluster.

CORE LOGIC:

The Cluster class initializes the clientProtocolProvider and client based on the configuration and jobTrackAddr. It also provides methods to get the file system, job, cluster status, task trackers, staging area directory, job history file path, queue ACLs, root queues, child queues, job tracker status, and task tracker expiry interval.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The Cluster class takes a Configuration object as input and provides methods to get the file system, job, cluster status, task trackers, staging area directory, job history file path, queue ACLs, root queues, child queues, job tracker status, and task tracker expiry interval as output.

DEPENDENCIES:

The Cluster class depends on the ClientProtocolProvider, ClientProtocol, UserGroupInformation, Configuration, FileSystem, Path, JobConf, JobID, TaskAttemptID, ClusterMetrics, TaskTrackerInfo, QueueInfo, QueueAclsInfo, JobStatus, and Token classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The Counter interface represents a named counter that tracks the progress of a map/reduce job.
- It has methods to set the display name of the counter, get the name and display name of the counter, get the current value of the counter, set the value of the counter, and increment the counter by a given value.

CORE LOGIC:
- The Counter interface is part of the Map-Reduce framework and is used to track the progress of a job.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The Counter interface does not have any specific inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The Counter interface does not have any specific dependencies.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

Holds per job/task counters, defined either by the Map-Reduce framework or applications.

CORE LOGIC:

Each Counter can be of any Enum type.
Counters are bunched into CounterGroups, each comprising of counters from a particular Enum class.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

Counters are bunched into CounterGroups.

DEPENDENCIES:

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The main purpose of this class is to manage and execute MapReduce jobs.
- It provides methods for setting up and monitoring the job, as well as for controlling its execution.

CORE LOGIC:
- The core logic of this class is to handle the submission, execution, and monitoring of MapReduce jobs.
- It includes methods for setting up the job, such as setting the input format, output format, mapper, reducer, and partitioner.
- It also includes methods for monitoring the job, such as checking its status, progress, and task completion events.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The inputs to this class include the configuration, file system, and other job-related information.
- The outputs include the updated configuration, job status, progress updates, and task completion events.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The dependencies of this class include the Cluster class, the JobSubmitter class, and other classes related to MapReduce execution.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- setupProgress(): returns the progress of the job's setup-tasks.
- isComplete(): checks if the job is finished or not.
- isSuccessful(): checks if the job completed successfully.
- killJob(): kills the running job.
- setPriority(): sets the priority of a running job.
- setPriorityAsInteger(): sets the priority of a running job.
- getTaskCompletionEvents(): gets events indicating completion (success/failure) of component tasks.
- killTask(): kills indicated task attempt.
- failTask(): fails indicated task attempt.
- getCounters(): gets the counters for this job.
- getTaskDiagnostics(): gets the diagnostic messages for a given task attempt.
- setNumReduceTasks(): sets the number of reduce tasks for the job.
- setWorkingDirectory(): sets the current working directory for the default file system.
- setInputFormatClass(): sets the InputFormat for the job.
- setOutputFormatClass(): sets the OutputFormat for the job.
- setMapperClass(): sets the Mapper for the job.
- setJarByClass(): sets the Jar by finding where a given class came from.

- setUser(): sets the reported username for this job.

- setMapOutputKeyClass(): sets the key class for the map output data.
- setMapOutputValueClass(): sets the value class for the map output data.
- setOutputKeyClass(): sets the key class for the job output data.
- setOutputValueClass(): sets the value class for job outputs.
- setCombinerKeyGroupingComparatorClass(): defines the comparator that controls which keys are grouped together for a single call to combiner.
- setSortComparatorClass(): defines the comparator that controls how the keys are sorted before they are passed to the Reducer.
- setGroupingComparatorClass(): defines the comparator that controls which keys are grouped together for a single call to Reducer.reduce().
- setJobName(): sets the user-specified job name.
- setSpeculativeExecution(): turns speculative execution on or off for this job.
- setMapSpeculativeExecution(): turns spec

The counters are used to track various metrics related to the execution of the job, such as the number of failed or killed maps and reduces, the total number of maps and reduces launched, and the number of maps and reduces that were local, rack-local, or other-local.

The counters are incremented and updated as the job progresses, and can be accessed and used by other parts of the job or by users.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The JobID class represents the immutable and unique identifier for a job. It consists of two parts: the jobtracker identifier and the job number. The jobtracker identifier is used to map the jobID to the jobtracker, and the job number is incremented for each new job.

CORE LOGIC:

The JobID class provides methods for constructing and parsing job IDs, as well as comparing and hashing job IDs. It also includes a method for appending additional information to the job ID string.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The JobID class reads and writes data using the DataInput and DataOutput interfaces. It also includes a method for reading and writing the job ID string.

DEPENDENCIES:

The JobID class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapred.ID class and the org.apache.hadoop.io.Text class. It also uses the java.text.NumberFormat class for formatting job IDs.

package org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce;

import org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceAudience;
import org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceStability;

/**
 * Used to describe the priority of the running job. 
 * DEFAULT : While submitting a job, if the user is not specifying priority,
 * YARN has the capability to pick the default priority as per its config.
 * Hence MapReduce can indicate such cases with this new enum.
 * UNDEFINED_PRIORITY : YARN supports priority as an integer. Hence other than
 * the five defined enums, YARN can consider other integers also. To generalize
 * such cases, this specific enum is used.
 */
@InterfaceAudience.Public
@InterfaceStability.Evolving
public enum JobPriority {
  VERY_HIGH,
  HIGH,
  NORMAL,
  LOW,
  VERY_LOW,
  DEFAULT,
  UNDEFINED_PRIORITY;
}


SUMMARY:

This code chunk defines an enumeration of job priorities for the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework. The JobPriority enum includes six priority levels: VERY_HIGH, HIGH, NORMAL, LOW, VERY_LOW, DEFAULT, and UNDEFINED_PRIORITY. The DEFAULT priority is used when

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this class is to handle the localization of resources for MapReduce jobs.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this class is to check if there are any limits set for resource localization. If there are limits, it checks if the total size of the resources exceeds the maximum size allowed. It also checks if the number of resources exceeds the maximum number allowed.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The inputs to this class are the configuration, collections of files, libjars, and archives, the job jar, and a map of URI to FileStatus. The output is a boolean value indicating whether the limits have been exceeded.

DEPENDENCIES:
This class depends on the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework and its dependencies.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

- JobStatus is a Java class that represents the status of a job.
- It contains information such as the job ID, progress, state, start time, user, queue, priority, job name, job file, tracking URL, and more.
- It also provides methods to set and get various attributes of the job status.

CORE LOGIC:

- The class includes methods to set and get the job's progress, state, start time, user, queue, priority, job name, job file, tracking URL, and more.
- It also includes methods to set and get the job's ACLs (access control lists) and scheduling information.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

- The class implements the Writable interface, which allows it to be serialized and deserialized.
- It can be written to and read from a DataOutput and DataInput stream, respectively.

DEPENDENCIES:

- The class depends on the following classes and interfaces:
  - JobID: represents a unique identifier for a job.
  - JobPriority: represents the priority of a job.
  - JobACL: represents the access control list for a job.
  - AccessControlList: represents a list of users and groups with access permissions.
  - State: represents the state of a job (e.g., running, succeeded, failed, etc.).
  - Writable: interface for classes that can be serialized and deserialized.
  - WritableFactories: factory class for creating instances of Writable classes.
  - WritableFactory: interface for creating instances of Writable classes.
  - StringInterner: class for interning strings.
  - Text: class for handling text data.
  - Math: class for mathematical operations.

This chunk of code is responsible for formatting the job status information into a string.

CORE LOGIC:
The code uses a StringBuilder to append the job status information, including setup progress, run state, start time, user name, priority, scheduling info, number of used and reserved slots, as well as memory usage information.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes the job status information as input and returns a formatted string as output.

DEPENDENCIES:
This code does not depend on any specific classes or methods.

The createSplitFiles method takes in a Path jobSubmitDir, Configuration conf, FileSystem fs, and an array of InputSplit splits. It creates a file and writes the splits to it. The writeJobSplitMetaInfo method takes in a FileSystem fs, Path filename, FsPermission p, int splitMetaInfoVersion, and an array of JobSplit.SplitMetaInfo allSplitMetaInfo. It creates a file and writes the splits meta-info to it.

CORE LOGIC:
The code includes methods for escaping and unescaping delimiters in counter strings, as well as methods for converting counter objects to and from compact strings.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes counter objects as input and produces compact strings as output.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the Apache Hadoop MapReduce library and the Apache Hadoop classification library.
